ddls:
CREATE TABLE "purchases" (
  "purchase_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the purchase */,
  "mnt_wines" INTEGER /* Amount spent on wines */,
  "mnt_fruits" INTEGER /* Amount spent on fruits */,
  "mnt_meat_products" INTEGER /* Amount spent on meat products */,
  "mnt_fish_products" INTEGER /* Amount spent on fish products */,
  "mnt_sweet_products" INTEGER /* Amount spent on sweet products */,
  "mnt_gold_prods" INTEGER /* Amount spent on gold products */,
  "num_deals_purchases" INTEGER /* Number of purchases made with discounts */,
  "num_web_purchases" INTEGER /* Number of purchases made through the web */,
  "num_catalog_purchases" INTEGER /* Number of purchases made through the catalog */,
  "num_store_purchases" INTEGER /* Number of purchases made in-store */,
  "num_web_visits_month" INTEGER /* Number of web visits in the last month */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the purchase */,
  "total_revenue" REAL /* Total revenue generated from the purchase */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purchase_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign response record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who received the campaign */,
  "accepted_cmp3" INTEGER /* Response to campaign 3 (1 if accepted, 0 if not) */,
  "accepted_cmp4" INTEGER /* Response to campaign 4 (1 if accepted, 0 if not) */,
  "accepted_cmp5" INTEGER /* Response to campaign 5 (1 if accepted, 0 if not) */,
  "accepted_cmp1" INTEGER /* Response to campaign 1 (1 if accepted, 0 if not) */,
  "accepted_cmp2" INTEGER /* Response to campaign 2 (1 if accepted, 0 if not) */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "campaign_channel" TEXT /* Channel used to deliver the campaign (e.g. email, social media) */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer responded to the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "complaints" (
  "complaint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each complaint */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who filed the complaint */,
  "complain" INTEGER /* Indicates if a complaint was filed (1 if yes, 0 if no) */,
  "dt_complain" TEXT /* Date when the complaint was filed */,
  "complaint_type" TEXT /* Type of complaint (e.g. product, service, billing) */,
  "complaint_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the complaint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("complaint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cost_revenue" (
  "cost_revenue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cost and revenue record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "z_cost_contact" INTEGER /* Cost of contacting the customer */,
  "z_revenue" INTEGER /* Revenue generated from the customer */,
  "customer_acquisition_cost" REAL /* Cost of acquiring the customer */,
  "customer_retention_cost" REAL /* Cost of retaining the customer */,
  "total_lifetime_value" REAL /* Total lifetime value of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cost_revenue_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "response" INTEGER /* Response to the last marketing campaign (1 if accepted, 0 if not) */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer responded to the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "social_media" (
  "social_media_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each social media record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "facebook" TEXT /* Facebook username */,
  "twitter" TEXT /* Twitter handle */,
  "instagram" TEXT /* Instagram username */,
  "linkedin" TEXT /* LinkedIn profile */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("social_media_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market trend */,
  "trend_name" TEXT /* Name of the market trend */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the market trend */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the market trend */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the market trend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id")
)
